name: Western Digital Corporation
summary: Western Digital Corporation (WDC, Information Technology) — composite stance CONSTRUCTIVE. Latest reported quarter FY2026 Q2: revenue $3.34B, net margin 96.0%. Valuation 14.0x forward P/E (-7% vs peers). Spot $484.28 (above 200-DMA).
bull: Profitability premium — net margin of 96.0% in the latest quarter sits well above the cross-sector ~10–15% benchmark, indicative of either structural pricing power or operating leverage from scale. Expect this to compress only modestly through-cycle.
Margin trajectory — net margin expanded +54.1pp over the last 4 quarters in the dataset. Management's cost-out commentary on recent calls is showing up in the P&L, not just the press release.

Trend regime constructive — spot $484.28 sits +118.7% above the 200-DMA ($221.42). The long-term moving average has been a reliable reference for institutional capital and remains supportive of the directional bias.
bear: 52-week extension — at 93% of the 52-week range, the stock is one bad print away from a 10–15% reset back into the middle of the range. Position size accordingly.
Options market pricing risk — front-month implied move of 9.7% reflects the buy-side hedging earnings/macro tail-risk. The premium is real and historically has been right more often than wrong.
trigger_up: Net margin expands above 97.5% — confirms pricing power and/or cost discipline thesis.
Management raises FY guide on next quarterly call — direct upward revision to consensus.
trigger_down: Net margin compresses below 94.0% — flags either input-cost pressure or competitive dynamics shifting unfavorably.
Management cuts FY guide on next quarterly call — direct downward revision; historically sees 5–15% drawdown on print.
Decisive break below the 52-week low at $49.84 — technical confirmation that the long-term trend has rolled over.
bottom_line: CONSTRUCTIVE.
